steps.title: Map the mystery and study
steps.content: The text first explains that scientists have long been perplexed by the unusually large size of P. seppenradensis. It then describes Soto, Price, and colleagues examining fossils and proposing an evolutionary explanation for that size.

steps.title: Match the structure
steps.content:
• Choice A: Mentions an assumption being overturned, but the text presents a mystery and a possible explanation.
  • Choice B: Describes the mystery and the research team's attempt to solve it.
  • Choice C: Overstates the role of comparing Montana and England fossils.
  • Choice D: Claims a new species was discovered, which the text does not say.
The answer is B.
